# Cloud-Native KB Ingestion for External Workspaces

## Context

Graduated from [Discussion #11623](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/discussions/11623) on 2026-05-19 after 4-cycle cross-family convergence (3/3 explicit `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` signals — see Signal Ledger). For v13 cloud deployments of Agent OS, external client workspaces must be able to ingest their own code into the Knowledge Base while continuing to leverage Neo's curated content (guides, ADRs, skills, tickets, demo apps). The current KB substrate hardcodes the assumption "source repo = host repo of the KB."

**Sibling of [Epic #9999](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/9999), not sub.** #9999 owns READ-side multi-tenancy (`memorySharing` enum, AgentIdentity provenance, RLS) + identity-on-write. This Epic owns the WRITE-side cross-repo INGESTION substrate — the missing pillar for v13 cloud deployments.

## Topology Anchor — Unified Chroma (ADR 0003)

Per [ADR 0003 — Chroma Topology Unified Only](../tree/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0003-chroma-topology-unified-only.md) (Accepted 2026-05-09, Resolves #11011, Unblocks v13 Substrate Migration):

- **One ChromaDB daemon** (KB substrate manages lifecycle; MC defers as downstream client via `engines.chroma` coordinates)
- **Two MCP servers** — KB MCP server + Memory Core MCP server — connect to the same Chroma instance
- **Three collections** within the same Chroma instance:
  - `knowledge-base` — owned by KB MCP server
  - `neo-agent-memory` — owned by MC MCP server
  - `neo-agent-sessions` — owned by MC MCP server
- `chromaUnified` flag is permanently REMOVED
- `ChromaLifecycleService` is observability-passthrough only (no daemon spawning)
- `HealthService` statically reports `'unified'`

**This Epic's tenant-isolation work** (Phase 0/1C/D) adds tenant-scoping metadata fields + filter logic to the `knowledge-base` collection ONLY. It does NOT:
- introduce a new Chroma instance
- alter the unified topology
- move Memory Core storage into KB or vice-versa
- share collections cross-server (`knowledge-base` stays KB-exclusive; `neo-agent-memory`/`neo-agent-sessions` stay MC-exclusive)

The `memorySharing` enum (`legacy | private | team`) from #10010 is reused as a POLICY pattern; KB's INFRASTRUCTURE (metadata fields + `where`-clause filter on the `knowledge-base` collection) is new work.

## The Problem

The configurability gap is mechanically located. Substrate audit at dev=`6f513ac24` confirms:

- Hardcoded source-class array in [`ai/services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.mjs:460-471`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.mjs#L460-L471)
- Per-Source-class hardcoded paths (e.g., [`ApiSource.sourceMap`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/ApiSource.mjs#L67-L73) maps `src/apps/examples/docs/app/ai` — Neo-specific)
- Per-Source-class hardcoded parser binding
- Single `aiConfig.neoRootDir` (one source repo per KB instance) propagated through [`ApiSource.mjs:101-105`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/ApiSource.mjs#L101-L105) (neoRootDir-relative chunk metadata) and [`SearchService.mjs:118-120`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/SearchService.mjs#L118-L120) (single-root path resolution)

External workspaces in this epic are Neo workspaces created via `npx neo app` (where `neo` is a `node_module` dependency rather than the repo) AND non-Neo repositories with ES5 / C++ / other-language code that needs to be discoverable by client agents through the same MCP server.

**Substrate-grounded blockers caught during Discussion (cross-family peer review compounds empirical anchor):**

- GPT Cycle 1: `importDatabase` ≠ ingest transport (RESTORE-only, skips re-embedding); single-root path determinism breaks cross-tenant; content-hash delta only deletes under full-corpus sync; MCP work-volume gate refuses bulk; Phase 1 carried implicit-contract-risk
- Gemini Cycle 1: `memorySharing` enum is Memory-Core-only today (0 KB hits via grep); cross-substrate pattern reuse ≠ infrastructure reuse
- GPT Cycle 2: write-side server-stamping invariant + spoof-rejection security boundary; `QueryService.mjs:116-128` builds Chroma `where` only from `type`, no tenant predicate

## The Architectural Reality

**Existing substrate touched** (consumer sweep from Discussion §6, expanded per peer cycles):

| Substrate | Role | Epic-side change |
|---|---|---|
| [`DatabaseService.createKnowledgeBase()`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.mjs#L453-L490) | Hardcoded source-class array orchestration | Replace with data-driven source registry |
| [`source/Base.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/Base.mjs) | Abstract `extract(writeStream, createHashFn)` contract | Preserved; substrate-correct extension point already clean |
| [`source/*.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/) (10 concrete sources) | Per-source paths + parser binding hardcoded | Externalize paths to config; data-driven registration |
| [`parser/SourceParser.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/SourceParser.mjs), [`DocumentationParser.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/DocumentationParser.mjs), [`TestParser.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/TestParser.mjs) | Acorn / markdown / test parsers | Default parsers shipped; client-side parsers emit `parsed-chunk-v1` |
| [`DatabaseService.manageDatabaseBackup({action: 'import'})`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.mjs#L265-L349) | RESTORE-only JSONL `{id, embedding, metadata, document}` | Reframed as `backup-record-v1` precedent; distinct from ingest contract |
| [`VectorService.mjs:188-274`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/VectorService.mjs#L188-L274) | Content-hash delta + embedding + #10572 MCP volume gate | New write-side `tenantId` stamping; existing delete-logic preserved for full-corpus path |
| [`QueryService.mjs:116-128`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/QueryService.mjs#L116-L128) | Builds Chroma `where` from `type` only | Inject tenant/visibility `where` from authenticated AgentIdentity context |
| [`SearchService.mjs:118-120`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/SearchService.mjs#L118-L120) | Single-`neoRootDir` path resolution for file hydration | Tenant-aware hydration via Q12 options (chunk-metadata-embedded vs server-mirror vs hybrid) |
| `memorySharing` enum (#10010) | Memory-Core-only today; KB has 0 references | PATTERN REUSED, INFRASTRUCTURE NEW — chunk metadata `tenantId` injection + retrieval-time filter |
| AgentIdentity (#9999, #10011) | Memory Core graph + RLS | PATTERN REUSED, INFRASTRUCTURE NEW — server-stamped chunk provenance |
| #10572 MCP work-volume gate | Refuses `viaMcp` syncs > `mcpSyncMaxChunks` | LOAD-BEARING — forces bulk facade structurally |

## The Fix — Five-Phase Decomposition (updated 2026-05-19 per operator-directed sub-decomposition; Phase 5 added when integration/unit test parity gap surfaced)

**Phase 0/1 Epic [#11625](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11625) — Ingestion Contract + Registry Extraction + KB Tenant Isolation (contracts before implementation; `memorySharing` pattern applied to the `knowledge-base` Chroma collection per ADR 0003 unified topology)**
- [#11629](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11629) Phase 0/1A — Ingestion contracts (parsed-chunk-v1 + backup-record-v1 schemas + path-identity tuple + tombstone spec)
- [#11630](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11630) Phase 0/1B — Source/Parser registry extraction + byte-equivalence fixture
- [#11631](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11631) Phase 0/1C — KB Tenant Isolation write-side (VectorService server-stamping + tenant-aware chunkId + spoof-rejection on the `knowledge-base` collection)
- [#11632](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11632) Phase 0/1D — KB Tenant Isolation read-side (QueryService/SearchService where-filter on `knowledge-base` collection) + fail-closed test suite

Standalone win: enables same-server custom workspaces without network substrate.

**Phase 2 Epic [#11626](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11626) — Ingestion Service + MCP Small-Batch Facade + Bulk Facade** (blocked-by Phase 0/1)
- [#11633](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11633) Phase 2A — KnowledgeBaseIngestionService core (orchestrator + validation)
- [#11634](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11634) Phase 2B — MCP facade ingestSourceFiles + #10572 gate threading
- [#11635](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11635) Phase 2C — Bulk facade (CLI ai:ingest-tenant + streaming)
- [#11636](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11636) Phase 2D — Q12 search-hydration mode resolution + SearchService implementation
- [#11637](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11637) Phase 2E — Q5 tenant config storage (Native Edge Graph extension)
- [#11638](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11638) Phase 2F — Test fixture infrastructure (synthetic external workspaces × 4) + E2E multi-tenant suite

**Phase 3 sub-ticket [#11627](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11627) — Cloud Deployment Guide + Worked Examples** (blocked-by Phase 2)
Single sub-ticket (same-layer doc work; PR-level decomposition sufficient). New guide tree `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/` (Overview, Configuration, CustomSources, CustomParsers, HookWiring, Security, MigrationPath) + minimal worked example external workspace.

**Phase 4 Epic [#11628](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628) — Operations + Observability for Cloud-Native Deployments** (NEW per operator post-graduation review; blocked-by Phase 2)
- [#11639](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11639) Phase 4A — Per-tenant ingestion observability daemon (extends KBRecorderService)
- [#11640](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11640) Phase 4B — Manifest reconciliation daemon
- [#11641](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11641) Phase 4C — Stale-chunk garbage collection daemon
- [#11642](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11642) Phase 4D — Operator alerting surface (telemetry thresholds → A2A + external notification)

Phase 4 was not in Discussion #11623 §7 original decomposition; surfaced 2026-05-19 during operator-directed post-graduation review (rationale: cloud deployments need operability substrate; KBRecorderService is daemon-adjacent and extensible).

**Phase 5 Epic [#11643](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11643) — Integration + Unit Test Parity with Memory Core** (NEW per operator post-graduation review; Phase 5A independent / 5B blocked-by Phase 0/1D / 5C cross-cutting)
- [#11644](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11644) Phase 5A — KB MCP Server Integration Test Parity (pre-Phase-0/1 shippable; parallels MC's RemoteMcpTransport / AuthRejection / OidcAuth / BackupRestoreWipe / HeartbeatPropagation specs)
- [#11645](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11645) Phase 5B — KB Tenant Isolation Integration Tests (blocked-by Phase 0/1D #11632; parallels MC's TeamPrivateRetrieval / CrossTenantIsolation specs)
- [#11646](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11646) Phase 5C — KB Unit Test Coverage Expansion (cross-cutting; close 32-vs-12 parity gap; target ≥25 specs ≈ 80% of MC's 32)

Phase 5 was not in Discussion #11623 §7 original; surfaced 2026-05-19 during operator's test-substrate review. Empirical parity gap V-B-A'd: MC has 32 unit specs + 8 integration specs; KB has 12 unit specs + 1 integration spec (`healthcheck.spec.mjs` per [#10805](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10805) Lane A — dual-server coverage). Operator framing: *"this epic needs new tests here. and /unit-tests too."*

## Acceptance Criteria

**Cross-phase ACs:**

- [ ] All 3 phase sub-tickets filed with explicit cross-references back to this Epic
- [ ] Phase 0/1 ships first (contracts before implementation); Phase 2 sub-ticket filed when Phase 0/1 lands
- [ ] Each phase has standalone test substrate (unit + integration + e2e where applicable)
- [ ] Cross-substrate consumer sweep (consumer sweep dimensions from Discussion §6) reflected in each phase's tests
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/` guide tree exists at Phase 3 closeout; cross-links to #9999, #10010, #10011, #10030

**Phase 0/1 ACs** (filed as sub-ticket):

- [ ] `parsed-chunk-v1` JSON Schema at `ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/parsed-chunk-v1.schema.json`
- [ ] `backup-record-v1` JSON Schema at `ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/backup-record-v1.schema.json` (formalizing existing `importDatabase` shape)
- [ ] Path-identity tuple `{tenantId, repoSlug, rootKind, sourcePath}` spec
- [ ] Tombstone / manifest / revision-boundary contract spec (mutually-supporting)
- [ ] Source/Parser registry extraction: hardcoded array → data-driven registry; `useDefaultSources` / `useDefaultParsers` boolean configs in `aiConfig`
- [ ] Per-source path externalization (`ApiSource.sourceMap` etc. → config)
- [ ] Custom-source / custom-parser registration API
- [ ] **memorySharing KB-side port (Write-side)** — `VectorService.embed` upsert path injects server-derived `{tenantId, visibility, originAgentIdentity?}` from authenticated AgentIdentity context; rejects or server-overwrites client-supplied tenant fields; tenant-aware `chunkId` hash derivation
- [ ] **memorySharing KB-side port (Read-side)** — `QueryService.queryDocuments` + `SearchService` inject `where: {tenantId: {$in: [<requester>, '<team-namespace>']}}` from server-side authenticated AgentIdentity, NOT client payload
- [ ] **Byte-equivalence fixture** — current Neo source output BEFORE registry extraction === output AFTER (verifies migration doesn't regress retrieval quality; adding `tenantId` field doesn't perturb chunk-hash semantics for existing content)
- [ ] **Fail-closed test suite** — forged client `tenantId`/`visibility` rejected/overwritten; tenant A cannot retrieve tenant B `private` chunks; Neo `team` chunks cross-tenant-visible; same `sourcePath` under two tenants isolated; spoof-rejection tested through every public KB query facade

**Phase 2 ACs** (deferred, filed as sub-ticket):

- [ ] `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService` singleton behind shared service layer
- [ ] MCP tool `ingestSourceFiles` accepts batches, gated by `aiConfig.mcpSyncMaxChunks` (#10572)
- [ ] Bulk facade (CLI `npm run ai:ingest-tenant <tenantId>` + HTTP/streaming)
- [ ] Tenant scoping via #9999 AgentIdentity substrate
- [ ] Server-side `parsed-chunk-v1` validation (rejects records with `embedding` field outside restore mode)
- [ ] Q12 hydration mode chosen and implemented before retrieval flow
- [ ] Density/UX measurement gate empirically defined

**Phase 3 ACs** (deferred, filed as sub-ticket):

- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/` guide tree (Overview, Configuration, CustomSources, CustomParsers, HookWiring, Security, MigrationPath)
- [ ] Minimal worked example external workspace
- [ ] Minimal `pre-push` hook in shell demonstrating `ingestSourceFiles` contract
- [ ] Guide validates referenced files exist; code examples are runnable

## Out of Scope

- Client-side embeddings (operator invariant; KB still owns embeddings)
- Pull-model (KB clones tenant repos) — inverts #9999 push-substrate identity direction
- Per-tenant Chroma storage split (rejected by #9999 Avoided Traps; re-audit only if cross-repo INGEST load empirically forces)
- Macro DB / full-file vectors (rejected by #10030)
- Server-side runtime parser execution from tenant-supplied code (untrusted-code risk; eliminated by client-side fallback for non-default parsers)
- Future WASM/tree-sitter sandboxing lane for server-side custom parsers — separate Discussion when needed

## Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

| Trap | Why rejected |
|---|---|
| Conflating `backup-record-v1` (restore) with ingest contract | `importDatabase` preserves embeddings, skips `TextEmbeddingService.embedTexts()` — distinct from server-embeds ingest path. Two distinct contracts. |
| Content-hash delta as sole deletion mechanism | Only works under full-corpus sync (`VectorService.mjs:198-207`); incremental push MUST use tombstone/manifest/revision-boundary |
| Pure-MCP transport for bulk imports | #10572 work-volume gate refuses syncs > `mcpSyncMaxChunks` via MCP; bulk facade is structurally necessary |
| Implicit single-`neoRootDir` source-path semantics | `SearchService.mjs:118-120` resolves against single root; cross-tenant needs explicit identity tuple |
| Assumed cross-substrate reuse of `memorySharing` enum | Memory-Core-only today (0 KB references); pattern reuse ≠ infrastructure reuse. Validate cross-substrate assumptions via grep, not memory of design-intent. |
| Client-supplied tenant/visibility trust | Server stamps from authenticated AgentIdentity; clients may not spoof. Load-bearing security invariant. |
| Conflating `memorySharing` enum (Memory Core policy pattern) with shared-DB topology (already unified per ADR 0003 since 2026-05-09) | KB + MC are SEPARATE MCP servers sharing ONE Chroma DB but maintaining SEPARATE collections. Phase 0/1 adds tenant-scoping metadata to KB's `knowledge-base` collection only; it does NOT alter unified topology or move storage between servers. Terminology: "KB Tenant Isolation (memorySharing pattern reuse)" — NOT "memorySharing KB Port" which suggests storage relocation. |

* feat(kb): scaffold Phase 0/1A ingestion contracts (#11629)

Add JSON Schemas + companion docs for the cross-tenant cloud ingestion
contracts introduced by Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion):

- parsed-chunk-v1.schema.json — client-side ingest contract; server embeds
  via TextEmbeddingService.embedTexts() in VectorService.embed; records
  carrying an embedding field are forbidden by design (they belong to the
  backup-record-v1 restore-only contract).
- backup-record-v1.schema.json — formalizes existing importDatabase shape
  (DatabaseService.mjs:250-349); restore-only, embedding-preserving;
  distinct contract from parsed-chunk-v1.
- identity-tuple.md — path-identity tuple {tenantId, repoSlug, rootKind,
  sourcePath} semantics; replaces single-neoRootDir assumption per ADR 0003
  unified Chroma topology (one daemon, three collections, separate MCP
  servers — KB writes to the knowledge-base collection only).
- deletion-signaling-contract.md — tombstone + manifest snapshot +
  revision-boundary mutually-supporting contract for incremental push
  deletion-signaling; complements (does not replace) the existing
  VectorService.mjs:198-207 full-corpus delete logic.
- source/Base.mjs JSDoc updated with cross-references to all four
  contracts + ADR 0003 topology anchor.

Schema unit tests (#11629 AC items 6-7) deferred to follow-up commit on
this branch.

* test(kb): add parsed-chunk-v1 + backup-record-v1 schema validation tests (#11629)

20 cases covering Phase 0/1A schema invariants via ajv (JSON Schema 2020-12):

parsed-chunk-v1:
- accepts fully-populated valid record
- accepts minimal record (only required fields)
- REJECTS records carrying an embedding field (spoof-rejection invariant
  backing the Phase 0/1C contract boundary; the embedding field is reserved
  for backup-record-v1 restore path and triggers schema-layer rejection
  here per additionalProperties: false)
- rejects records missing required fields
- rejects mismatched schemaVersion (const: '1.0.0')
- rejects invalid rootKind enum values (neo-workspace | bare-repo | external-source)
- rejects tenantId with uppercase characters (AgentIdentity slug convention)
- rejects empty hashInputs array (deterministic chunkId needs >= 1 input)
- rejects unknown top-level fields
- accepts open-enum kind values (parser-protocol extensibility regression-guard)
- JSON round-trip preserves validity

backup-record-v1:
- accepts valid record with embedding
- accepts valid record WITHOUT embedding (optional; Chroma can re-embed)
- rejects records missing required id / metadata / document fields
- rejects unknown top-level fields
- JSON round-trip preserves validity

Schema contract separation:
- valid parsed-chunk-v1 record is NOT a valid backup-record-v1 record
- valid backup-record-v1 record is NOT a valid parsed-chunk-v1 record

Test fixture lives at test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/
following the AI hemisphere convention from learn/benefits/ArchitectureOverview.md
+ unit-test-workflow.md §7 directory conventions.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* fix(kb): tighten backup-record-v1 + Base.mjs JSDoc per Cycle 1 review (#11629)

Resolve substrate-correct REQUEST_CHANGES from GPT Cycle 1 /pr-review on PR
#11647 (PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAXcdBQ).

backup-record-v1.schema.json:
- `embedding` field changed from OPTIONAL to REQUIRED (added to required[],
  minItems: 1)
- Description rewritten to reflect actual DatabaseService.importDatabase
  runtime constraint: importDatabase passes `embeddings: batch.map(r => r.embedding)`
  into collection.upsert(); missing embedding produces an undefined slot in the
  embeddings array, NOT an omitted property. Chroma auto-embed only fires when
  the entire recordSet.embeddings property is absent, not when individual slots
  are undefined. The schema now matches what restore actually supports.
- No-embedding restore support is documented as out-of-scope for #11629; it
  would require runtime changes to importDatabase to omit the embeddings field
  conditionally.

Schemas.spec.mjs:
- Replaced 'accepts a valid record WITHOUT embedding' test with its inverse:
  'REJECTS records missing embedding field (matches DatabaseService.importDatabase
  runtime constraint)'. Same total count (20 cases). New test asserts schema-layer
  rejection of records that runtime cannot actually restore, eliminating the
  prior contract drift between AJV acceptance and importDatabase behavior.

source/Base.mjs JSDoc:
- Tightened class-level JSDoc. Sources emit `parsed-chunk-v1` ONLY. The
  `backup-record-v1` sibling contract is described as restore-only, produced
  by DatabaseService.manageDatabaseBackup({action: 'export'}) and consumed by
  importDatabase — NOT emitted by any source's extract(). Closes the
  rhetorical-drift Required Action surfaced by GPT's Cycle 1 review.

Contract Ledger (T3) backfilled on issue #11629 body in a separate ticket-body
edit (no diff in this PR's tree).

Test plan:
- npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/parser/Schemas.spec.mjs
  → 20 passed (722ms)

* feat(ai): KB Source/Parser registry + useDefaultSources/useDefaultParsers configs (#11658)

Phase 0/1B substrate of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion). Replaces the
hardcoded source-list at `DatabaseService.createKnowledgeBase` with a data-driven
registry that supports:

- Neo defaults auto-registered via `./source/_export.mjs` when
  `aiConfig.useDefaultSources !== false`.
- Tenant-supplied custom sources/parsers registered declaratively via
  `aiConfig.customSources` / `aiConfig.customParsers` OR programmatically via
  `SourceRegistry.registerSource(...)` / `registerParser(...)`.
- Idempotent re-registration (same name overwrites prior class — useful for
  hot-reload during development without registry drift).

New surfaces:

- `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/SourceRegistry.mjs` — singleton with
  registerSource/registerParser/getSources/getParsers/has*/unregister*/clear API.
- `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/_export.mjs` — auto-registration of Neo's
  10 default Source classes in the same insertion order as the pre-#11658
  hardcoded array (byte-equivalence anchor: AdrSource → ApiSource → ConceptSource
  → DiscussionSource → LearningSource → PullRequestSource → ReleaseNotesSource →
  SkillSource → TicketSource → TestSource).
- `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.{mjs,template.mjs}` — new keys:
  `useDefaultSources: true`, `useDefaultParsers: true`, `customSources: []`,
  `customParsers: []`.

DatabaseService refactor: imports `./source/_export.mjs` (which triggers auto-
registration as a side effect of the registry-singleton initialization), then
iterates `SourceRegistry.getSources()` instead of the hardcoded array.

Backwards compat: existing config.mjs files without the new keys fall through
to truthy defaults — pre-existing behavior preserved. Empirical verification:
8 existing Source + DatabaseService.backup tests pass alongside the new 11
SourceRegistry tests. Total: 19 tests, 0 regressions.

Per-source path externalization (sourcePaths config) deferred to Phase 0/1B-β
follow-up — this PR ships the registry foundation; per-source paths are a
purely additive layer above it. #11658 remains open until the path-externalization
slice lands. (#11658)

* fix(ai): refactor _export.mjs into testable applyConfigToRegistry fn + config-driven tests (#11658)

Addresses 2 of 3 Required Actions from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11659
(commentId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAY3bSg):

**RA2 — Direct coverage for config-driven registration path:**
Extracted the import-time auto-registration into an exported pure function
`applyConfigToRegistry(registry, config, {defaults})`. The function is invoked
once at module-load time with the production aiConfig (preserves zero-config
default-register-on-import shape); tests now exercise the same logic with mocked
config shapes against a fresh registry instance. 7 new tests cover:
- `useDefaultSources: false` skips defaults (Required Action 2 acceptance criterion)
- `useDefaultSources` truthy default registers all 10 sources
- Declarative `customSources` entry with explicit `sourceName` override
- Declarative `customSources` entry deriving sourceName from className final segment
- Declarative `customParsers` entry with explicit `parserId` override
- Defensive skip on malformed customSources entries (null entry, missing SourceClass, falsy SourceClass)
- Full cloud-deployment shape: defaults disabled + 2 tenant sources + 1 tenant parser

**RA3 — Fix JSDoc shape mismatch:**
The `_export.mjs` module JSDoc previously described `customSources = [{className,
sourceName, ...}, ...]` while the implemented + configured shape is `{SourceClass,
sourceName?}`. The class object (not a className string) is passed via `SourceClass`
— preserving Neo's class-extension semantics. JSDoc now correctly documents the
implemented shape with a worked code example.

**RA1 (close-target semantics) handled separately** via PR body edit (change
`Resolves #11658` → `Related: #11658` so the ticket remains open for the deferred
per-source-path AC). No code change required for RA1.

Test verification: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/SourceRegistry.spec.mjs`
→ 18 passed in 715ms (11 originals + 7 new). (#11658)

- 2026-05-20T06:01:26Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5d64a1f` - "feat(ai): KB ingestion telemetry schema + recordIngestionMetric API (#11639) (#11667)

* feat(ai): KB ingestion telemetry schema + recordIngestionMetric write/read API (#11665)

Phase 4A schema slice of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion), sub-ticket
of Phase 4 #11628. Ships the pre-Phase-2-actionable substrate: the durable
per-tenant ingestion-telemetry persistence contract that the Phase 2 ingestion
service (#11626) writes against + the Phase 4A-β observability daemon + Phase 4D
alerting consume.

**Scope correction (documented in ADR 0013 §3.4):** #11665 originally scoped the
observability DAEMON into this PR. Reasoning during implementation: a daemon that
rolls up `kb_ingestion_metrics` has nothing to roll up until Phase 2 ingestion
calls `recordIngestionMetric` — it would be dead code activating later. The
schema + write/read APIs have standalone pre-Phase-2 value. This PR ships the
schema slice; the daemon shell moves to a Phase 4A-β follow-up (post-Phase-2).
#11665 stays open via `Related:` for that remaining slice.

**Surfaces:**

- `ai/services/knowledge-base/KBRecorderService.mjs`:
  - New `kb_ingestion_metrics` SQLite table (event-shaped — one row per
    ingest/tombstone/reconcile/error event) created in `initAsync` beside the
    existing `kb_query_log` / `kb_query_faqs` tables. Indexed on tenant_id /
    timestamp / event_type.
  - `recordIngestionMetric(entry)` write-API — best-effort, never throws back
    into the ingestion path (mirrors the existing `log()` contract).
  - `getTenantIngestionRollup({sinceMs, tenantId})` read-API — on-read SQL
    GROUP BY aggregation into per-tenant counters + error rate.
- `learn/agentos/decisions/0013-kb-ingestion-telemetry-schema.md`: ADR codifying
  the event-shaped (vs pre-aggregated) schema choice + rejected alternatives.

**Why event-shaped not pre-aggregated** (ADR 0013 §3.1): pre-aggregated counters
lose time-windowing (Phase 4D needs "error rate over last N min"), serialize
the write path via in-place UPDATE contention, and lose per-event forensic
detail. Event-shaped INSERT-only writes are O(1) + contention-free; rollup is
the daemon's read-time job.

Tests: new `KBRecorderService.ingestionMetrics.spec.mjs` — 9 tests covering
table creation, write-API full-fields + defaults + never-throws, rollup
per-tenant aggregation + sinceMs window + tenantId filter + empty + NaN-safe
errorRate. All 9 pass; existing 3 KBRecorderService tests pass as regression.

(#11665)

* fix(ai): null KBRecorderService.db after close + ADR Related-not-Resolves wording (#11665)

Addresses both Required Actions from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11667
(reviewId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAcVsyg):

**RA1 — cross-spec singleton-state leak:**
`KBRecorderService.ingestionMetrics.spec.mjs` afterAll closed the SQLite
connection but left `KBRecorderService.db` pointing at it. `initAsync` short-
circuits on `if (this.db) return`, so a sibling KBRecorderService spec running
later in the same worker inherited the closed connection and failed with
"The database connection is not open" at KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:50.
GPT's same-worker reproduction (`--workers=1` with both specs) caught it.
Fix: `KBRecorderService.db = null` after `.close()`, mirroring the existing
`KBRecorderService.spec.mjs` cleanup. Verified — the same-worker reproduction
now passes 12/12.

**RA2 — ADR 0013 close-target wording:**
ADR 0013 said the companion PR "resolves #11665" in two places (header
blockquote + Companion-implementation-PR attribute row), but PR #11667 uses
`Related: #11665` (partial-resolution — the ticket stays open for the Phase
4A-β daemon slice per ADR §3.4). Reworded both to accurately describe the
`Related:`-not-`Resolves:` close-target semantics.

No production-code change — both RAs are test-cleanup + doc-wording. (#11665)

* docs(adr): re-home ADR 0013 ticket refs to canonical #11639 (#11639)

#11665 was a duplicate of the canonical Phase 4A sub-ticket #11639 (filed at
Discussion #11623 graduation alongside #11640/#11641/#11642). #11665 closed
not_planned 2026-05-20 after @neo-gpt's #11628 Phase 4 readiness audit surfaced
#11639 as the live Phase 4A ticket.

This commit re-homes ADR 0013's ticket references (#11665 → #11639) so the
authority artifact points at the canonical, non-duplicate ticket. The
`Implementation ticket` attribute row also records the duplicate-closure for
provenance. No schema/API change — pure ticket-reference correctness fix.

- 2026-05-20T07:48:25Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d1b3cf7` - "feat(ai): externalize per-source paths to aiConfig.sourcePaths (#11660) (#11661)

* feat(ai): externalize per-source paths to aiConfig.sourcePaths (#11660)

Phase 0/1B-β of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion). Closes the deferred AC
from #11658 (PR #11659) — per-source path externalization across the 10 default
Neo Source classes. Cloud deployments where the tenant's filesystem layout differs
from Neo's can now reuse Neo's curated Source classes while overriding their input
paths via `aiConfig.sourcePaths.<SourceName>`. #11658 stays open until this PR
merges, then can be closed as fully resolved.

**Surfaces touched:**

- `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.template.mjs` (canonical) + paired
  gitignored `config.mjs`: new `sourcePaths` config object with all 10 Neo
  source defaults pre-populated.
- 10 Source classes refactored to read `aiConfig.sourcePaths?.<SourceName> ??
  '<hardcoded-fallback>'` at each path-resolution point. Hardcoded fallback
  preserves byte-equivalence for pre-#11660 deployments whose local config.mjs
  was generated before this commit (the `??` rhs IS the legacy default).

**Shape variation per source class:**

- **Single-path string** (AdrSource, ConceptSource, ReleaseNotesSource,
  SkillSource, TestSource, LearningSource): `'learn/agentos/decisions'` etc.
- **Array of paths** (DiscussionSource, PullRequestSource, TicketSource):
  `['primary', 'archive']` — supports cloud deployments without an archive
  subdir (single-element arrays work).
- **Object map** (ApiSource): `{path: type}` sourceMap-style for multi-directory
  indexing.

Each Source class interprets its own entry shape; the config is just a
per-source override surface.

**LearningSource special-case:** the config entry points at the tree.json file;
the base directory containing the .md files is derived as the path's containing
directory (so a cloud deployment can put guides under `docs/guides/tree.json`
+ `docs/guides/*.md` without changing the Source class).

**Tests:**

- New spec `SourcePathsConfig.spec.mjs` — 36 tests covering: byte-equivalence
  anchor (default config matches each hardcoded fallback exactly), override
  precedence, missing-key fallthrough, defensive whole-object-deletion
  fallthrough, LearningSource base-derivation semantics.
- Regression: 30 pre-existing source + DatabaseService.backup tests pass; 18
  SourceRegistry tests pass; full 66-test source-tree run zero failures
  (after re-run confirmed earlier interleaving flake was transient).

(#11660)

* fix(ai): serialize SourcePathsConfig spec for fullyParallel local execution (#11660)

Addresses Required Action 1 from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11661
(formal reviewId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAcNngw, lightweight hold commentId
IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9dlIg): the spec mutates the shared `aiConfig.sourcePaths`
singleton via per-test override + delete patterns. Under local `fullyParallel`
(workers=9), the per-test try/finally restoration windows overlap and cause
cross-test races where one test's `delete aiConfig.sourcePaths[X]` is observed
by a concurrent test as missing.

CI uses `workers: 1` per playwright.config.unit.mjs which masks the issue at
the CI surface, but locally `npm run test-unit -- <single-file>` ran 9 workers
and exposed the race.

Fix: `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` at the top of the spec file.
Tests now run sequentially within the worker, eliminating the overlap window.
Verified: 36 passed in 599ms after the change.

Same pattern as `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md` memory anchor:
"Playwright fullyParallel interleaves specs in same worker; cross-singleton
mutations need symmetric beforeEach+afterEach resets, not just beforeEach."
My in-test try/finally was the symmetric reset but couldn't compensate for
concurrent-window overlap; serial mode is the correct shape.

(#11660)

* fix(ai): byte-equivalence anchors text-grep template instead of live config (#11660)

Addresses Cycle 1 re-review hold from @neo-gpt commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9gB_A
(formal Cycle 2 still pending in-progress CI on cbdd01806):

GPT's clone's gitignored `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` was created
before this PR's template update + the clone hadn't re-run `bootstrapWorktree.mjs`
to refresh. The spec's byte-equivalence anchor assertions
(`expect(aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]).toBe(fallback)`) were coupled to local
config-freshness — passing on fresh clones, failing on stale clones.

Root cause: the spec's `aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]` assertion conflated two
distinct concerns:
  1. Stale-clone protection (the `??` fallback in Source classes works regardless)
  2. Cross-file drift detection (Source-class inline fallback vs template default)

Fixed:

- Byte-equivalence anchor reframed as a text-grep against the canonical git-tracked
  template file (`fs.readFile(TEMPLATE_PATH)` + `expect(templateText).toContain(...)`).
  This is stale-clone-safe (the template is git-tracked, never stale) and still
  catches Source-class-inline-fallback vs template-default drift if either side
  changes without the other.
- Direct `templateConfig` module import was attempted but triggers `Neo.setupClass`
  namespace collision under `unitTestMode` (both config.mjs + config.template.mjs
  register the same className). Text-grep avoids the namespace registry entirely.
- Override + missing-key runtime tests still mutate `aiConfig.sourcePaths` to verify
  resolution behavior. Added defensive `if (!aiConfig.sourcePaths) aiConfig.sourcePaths = {}`
  in beforeAll so stale-clone fresh test runs don't crash on the mutations. afterAll
  restores the original state (undefined or otherwise) so clone-freshness leaks
  don't propagate to subsequent specs.

Test verification: 36 passed in 631ms post-fix.

Substrate-evolution insight: the byte-equivalence anchor design is the right
intent (detect cross-file drift) but the implementation has to be careful about
what it's actually asserting. Asserting on the live config conflates "fresh
clone" with "no drift". Asserting on the canonical template separates concerns.

(#11660)

* feat(kb): add ai:ingest-tenant bulk-facade CLI (#11635)

Phase 2C of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion). Adds the bulk
data-plane facade for initial tenant onboarding + large hook bursts that
exceed the #10572 MCP work-volume gate.

- buildScripts/ai/ingestTenant.mjs - new `npm run ai:ingest-tenant` CLI:
  streams parsed-chunk-v1 JSONL (file or stdin), batches (default 500),
  calls KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.ingestSourceFiles with viaMcp:false,
  wrapped in the shared heavy-maintenance lease. structural-pre-flight:
  placed in buildScripts/ai/ alongside the KB data-plane scripts
  (syncKnowledgeBase / backup / restore), camelCase per the sibling
  convention.
- KnowledgeBaseIngestionService - thread a caller-selected `viaMcp` seam
  through ingestSourceFiles -> embedChunkGroups -> VectorService.embed
  instead of hardcoding viaMcp:true (the #11679-fold). Omitted/truthy =
  MCP-safe default (gate applies); explicit false = bulk CLI bypass.

Contract Ledger in the #11635 intake comment. Tests (unit + integration)
follow in a subsequent commit on this branch.

(the bulk CLI gate-bypass), an omitted viaMcp defaults to the MCP-safe
true, and an explicit viaMcp:true is preserved (the MCP facade path).

  readable stream directly (tests drive it with an in-memory Readable);

  covering arg parsing, JSONL stream parsing (incl. malformed-line
  parseError without aborting), batching into batchSize groups, parse-
  error counting, per-batch summary-error propagation, and the empty-
  stream zero-summary shape. 9/9 pass.

end-to-end CLI ingest with Chroma state verification.

- test/playwright/integration/KBBackupRestoreWipe.integration.spec.mjs:
  new `ingestTenantViaCli` helper + test case. Streams a 1024-record
  parsed-chunk-v1 JSONL fixture through the CLI's exported orchestration
  units (readJsonlRecords -> runIngest, batchSize 500) wired to the real
  KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.ingestSourceFiles({viaMcp:false}) bulk
  path, into an isolated temp Chroma collection. Asserts batch count
  (ceil(1024/500)=3), full-count ingest + embeddingsGenerated, and the
  Chroma ground-truth count - the 1024-chunk corpus far exceeds
  mcpSyncMaxChunks, so a clean ingest proves the #10572 gate bypass.

The ingestion service is imported directly (not via ai/services.mjs)
so the closure-injected viaMcp:false survives the makeSafe Zod wrapper.
bucket-D Docker test: validated by CI's integration-unified job (no
Docker in the authoring sandbox).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* docs(kb): correct ai:ingest-tenant npm invocation to require -- separator (#11635)

The CLI `Usage:` JSDoc line and the `printUsage()` help string advertised
`npm run ai:ingest-tenant <tenantId> ...`, but npm does not forward
positional args to the script without the `--` separator — `npm run
ai:ingest-tenant <tenantId>` leaves `process.argv.slice(2)` empty, so the
CLI prints usage and exits before `parseArgs` ever sees the tenant id.

Both strings now advertise the working form `npm run ai:ingest-tenant --
<tenantId> (--from-file <path.jsonl> | --from-stdin) [--batch-size <n>]`.
Implementation logic is unchanged — the parser was always correct; only
the documented invocation contract was wrong.

Per @neo-gpt's PR #11697 Cycle-1 review (REQUEST_CHANGES, 1 Required Action).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-20T22:59:40Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11700
- 2026-05-20T23:33:06Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11704
- 2026-05-21T00:32:18Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11703
- 2026-05-21T00:41:25Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `17fa1c3` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomParsers cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — second of the four
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide documents.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomParsers.md — how a cloud tenant
turns its own file formats into Knowledge Base chunks:

- The parsed-chunk-v1 ingest contract — required/optional fields, the
  forbidden embedding field, hashInputs semantics, additionalProperties:false.
- Client-side vs server-side parsing as a trust decision — client-side
  (push parsedChunks; the only path for non-JS formats; no code runs on the
  deployment) vs server-side (registered parser class, operator-gated).
- Authoring a server-side parser — registration via aiConfig.customParsers /
  SourceRegistry.registerParser; the parseIngestionFile(file,{tenantContext})
  modern contract vs the legacy parse() contract the built-in parsers use;
  KB_PARSER_NOT_REGISTERED + the raw-text fallback.
- The parser-execution trust boundary, cross-linked to Security.md.

Endpoint-exact — verified against the merged parsed-chunk-v1 schema, the
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks dispatch ladder, SourceRegistry,
and the built-in SourceParser/DocumentationParser precedent. The remaining
Phase 3B docs (CustomSources, Configuration) + worked examples land in
follow-up commits on this branch; #11627 stays open until the PR completes.

configuration surface:

- The two configuration layers — deployment-wide aiConfig (config.mjs) vs
  per-tenant KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig graph storage.
- The cloud-relevant aiConfig keys — useDefaultSources/useDefaultParsers,
  customSources/customParsers, sourcePaths; defaultTenantId/defaultRepoSlug/

  mcpHttpPort/publicUrl/auth for SSE cloud deployments — each with defaults.
- Per-tenant config storage (#11637): the KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig node,

  setTenantConfig versioning + RLS gate, the kb-config.yaml bootstrap, the

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T00:55:52Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `59ebc23` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomSources cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the fourth and final
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide document.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomSources.md — authoring a custom
Source class to index a new content territory in the full-corpus build:

- Source vs Parser — when each applies; most cloud tenants need Custom
  Parsers (push path); Custom Sources serve the full-corpus build path.
- The Source contract — extends source/Base.mjs, async extract(writeStream,
  createHashFn): Promise<Number>, the JSONL chunk-record shape ({type, kind,
  name, content, source} + chunk.hash = createHashFn(chunk)), documented per
  the verified AdrSource precedent.
- A worked custom Source example modelled on AdrSource.
- Registration via aiConfig.customSources / SourceRegistry.registerSource;
  the useDefaultSources gate.
- Path conventions (aiConfig.sourcePaths) + identity-tuple semantics — the
  Source emits content; createHashFn + the write-side stamp own identity.

Documents the extract contract from the verified built-in-Source precedent
(AdrSource + DatabaseService.createContentHash), distinct from the
parsed-chunk-v1 push contract. Completes the four-file Phase 3B guide set;
remaining Phase 3B work — the examples/cloud-deployment/ worked example,
learn/tree.json integration, and the Phase 3A forward-pointer refresh —
lands in follow-up commits before the PR opens. #11627 stays open.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T01:05:29Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `d3cd505` - "docs(examples): add Phase 3B cloud-deployment worked examples (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the runnable worked
examples accompanying the learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/ guide tree.

Adds examples/cloud-deployment/:
- minimal-external-workspace/ — an external tenant workspace (neo.mjs as a
  dependency): package.json, a sample proto/example.proto, src/ProtoParser.mjs
  (custom Parser — parseIngestionFile -> parsed-chunk-v1, the push path),
  src/ProtoSource.mjs (custom Source — extract(), the full-corpus path), and a
  README documenting registration + the smoke-test path.
- pre-push-hook.sh — a git pre-push hook streaming a push's changed files into
  the KB via the ai:ingest-tenant bulk facade; deletion signaling marked as the
  documented HookWiring.md extension point.
- README.md — the example-set overview.

The custom Source/Parser are contract-faithful — ProtoParser emits all 11
parsed-chunk-v1 required fields (no embedding); ProtoSource follows the verified
AdrSource extract precedent. Syntax-verified: node --check on both .mjs files,
bash -n on the hook, valid package.json.

Completes the #11627 guide-tree + examples authoring. The remaining Phase 3B
step — learn/tree.json integration + the Phase 3A forward-pointer refresh —
lands in a follow-up commit; #11627 stays open until the PR.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* docs(examples): correct the minimal-workspace smoke-test parser contract (#11627)

PR #11707 Cycle-1 review (@neo-gpt RA2): the minimal-external-workspace README
smoke-test claimed a raw .proto push would use the server-side ProtoParser, but
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks defaults a file with no parserId
to the raw-text fallback (one whole-file chunk).

Reworks the Smoke test section to be accurate: the no-parserId push exercises
the raw-text fallback (one chunk, no registration needed); a second example
adds parserId: "proto" + the ProtoParser registration for per-protobuf-message
chunking. No prose now claims a chunking behavior the dispatch ladder does not
perform for the shown command.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* docs(examples): correct the pre-push hook's raw-text parser comment (#11627)

PR #11707 Cycle-2 review (@neo-gpt RA-A): the pre-push-hook.sh comment said
"the server parses raw files with a registered parser" — carrying the same
parser-selection misconception Cycle-1 RA2 corrected in the workspace README.
The hook emits {sourcePath, content} records with no parserId, so
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks applies the raw-text fallback
(one whole-file chunk); it does not select a registered parser by extension.

Reworks the comment to match the emitted payload: the no-parserId stream uses
the raw-text fallback, and a deployment with a registered parser adds the
matching parserId to chunk per that parser's contract.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>
